title: Not for me
content:
I wanted to start using it but it insisted on taking me on a simple tour ...Onece rady I tried to drag my file into it ...And nothing ! 
That showed me allready that this program dont use common interface standards at all.
If you like coders who want to reinvent the world of tag editing go for it. (I dont! Im a Music Producer)
Lastly this is NOT free! It wants you to register in order to get a trial.

author: _abl_
title: user Beware
content: This app will change various media and file types to show up as belonging to THIS app, after it's installed. Worse yet, this App has about a 60% success rate at its core task (editing MP3 tags). The tags didn't seem to stick, or didn't seem to get read after the fact in certain scenarios.  So - buyer beware.

author: spammy the pig
title: horrible
content: Tried purchasing the free version. I uploaded a song and changed the metadata. That was easy. Had no way of getting the song out of the app onto my desktop so I can upload it to dropbox and other places. No information out there. Google's AI assistant says you need the Paid version. I opted for the one year. APPLE asked me to confirm with my fingerprint I did and I heard the ding. Then the app quit. Ridiculous. I opened up the app again and it still said that I needed to pay because I had the free version. I then restarted the computer deleted the app and re-downloaded it. Same thing. OK, I thought… I guess it didn't take. I paid for it again using my finger and I heard the ding. Same thing happened. Forget this company at all costs.

author: badprogger
title: Evertag is the perfect music tags editor!
content:
If you’re someone like me with a huge music library, organizing it can be a pain. That’s where Evertag comes in—it makes editing and managing music tags incredibly easy. I’ve been using it on my Mac for a while, and it’s been a game-changer for keeping my library clean and well-organized.

Key features that make Evertag stand out:

- Batch editing: One of my favorite features! I can edit tags for multiple files at once, saving me tons of time. Whether I’m updating artist names, album titles, or genres, it’s fast and efficient.

- Supports all major audio formats: I work with MP3, FLAC, and WAV files, and Evertag handles them all without breaking a sweat. It’s perfect for those with diverse music collections.

- Auto tag recognition: The app can automatically find and update missing tags. It scans the file’s metadata and fills in all the blanks—album art, release year, you name it.

- Album art downloader: Finding and embedding high-quality album artwork is super easy with Evertag. It can pull cover art from online databases, so my library looks as good as it sounds.

- Clean and simple interface: I really appreciate how intuitive the interface is. It’s straightforward to navigate, even if you’re not familiar with music tagging apps.

- Tag customization: If you’re particular about organizing your music, this app gives you complete control over every detail—from track numbers to comments and lyrics.

Evertag has made organizing my music library on Mac a breeze. The batch editing and auto tag recognition are lifesavers, and it’s clear the developers really understand what music lovers need. If you want a reliable and powerful music tag editor, I highly recommend giving Evertag a try!

author: Hearts&Thoughts
title: Too buggy
content: This app was so buggy.  First, when I point it at the folder I want to organize and tag, it loads all of the files out of order even though they are numbered.  It let me batch edit the album title and artist.  When I tried to single edit each song, it kept changing the other songs too as though batch edit was still activated.  I tried a few different things to try stopping it from doing it, but gave up after a while.  Waste of time.  iTunes used to do this very well.  No idea why tagging is so difficult now

Gott sei Dank nur ein Monatsabo gekauft

author: scofiel9
title: Inconsistent and a bit clunky
content: I really like the idea of the program, but found it cumbersome and inconsistent. Sometimes it saved the metadata. Sometimes it didn't. I tried copying to different folders, re-entering information. I could see the metadata in the program, but it didn't show in finder. I tried 4 similar files and only two I could get to work. A bit about the workflow. They create their own folders, copies within those folders. You can put them back, but it is cumbersome.  I wish you could choose the file, change the metadata, and create a new copy it its current location.

author: BoonX23
title: L APP CHE CERCAVO!
content:
Sono un tipo puntiglioso su ordine ed oraganizzazione sopratutto per quanto riguarda la musica..mi piace ascoltare la musica ed infatti nel tempo ho accumulato una quantità spropositata di brani di tutti i generi. 
organizzare tutta i meta dati con le relative copertine degli album riuscivo a farlo con una vecchia versione di aimp... adesso grazie a questa app posso fare tutto tranquillamente da mac in tutta semplicità, aggiungendo il titolo, il cantante e sopratutto la copertina dell' album con un clic!
Era l'app che stavo cercando!
Se non sbaglio oltre a modificare i dati del singolo brano, credo che si possa ascoltare un brano in anteprima!! Ottimo la consilglio

author: YannisOc
title: Good App needs a bit more development
content:
The app is great in general. The feature of getting the data from the net for the track works well and 95% ok (some covers are off).
However there must be a way to edit multiple tracks together and maybe see them on a list.
There must be a way also to get data from the internat for multiple tacks and save them all together.
The one by one method of importing a track-getting data from net and saving gets quite tiring.
Great work, keep developing
